title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Section Test

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding successive changes in percentage-based values: I. If a value is increased by 30% and then decreased by 30%, the net change is always negative. II. Successive percentage increase of 20% and 25% is equal an increase of 50%. III. The net percentage change after successive operations can be found using the formula: Net % Change = x + y + (xy100\frac{xy}{100}100xy‚Äã‚Äã) Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Given: We are given four statements regarding successive percentage changes. We need to validate each of them and determine which ones are correct. Formula Used: Successive Percentage Change Formula: Net % Change = x + y + xy100\frac{xy}{100}100xy‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Statement I: If a value is increased by 30% and then decreased by 30%: Net % Change = 30 - 30 + 30√ó‚àí30100\frac{30√ó-30}{100}10030√ó‚àí30‚Äã‚Äã = 0 - 9 = -9% => Net decrease of 9%. So, Statement I is correct. Statement II: Successive increase of 20% and 25% Net % Change = 20 + 25 + 20√ó25100\frac{20√ó25}{100}10020√ó25‚Äã‚Äã = 45 + 5 = 50% => Same as direct 50% increase, so this statement is correct. Statement III: The formula used for successive percentage change = x + y + xy100\frac{xy}{100}100xy‚Äã‚Äã which is valid for all successive changes. => So, Statement III is correct.

questions.question: In a triangle ABC, one of the angles is average of the remaining two angles. Which of the following is always true about the triangle ABC?
questions.explanation: Given: Triangle ABC with one angle equal to the average of the other two. Solution: Let the three angles be A, B, C. Suppose A =B+C2.= \dfrac{B + C}{2}.=2B+C‚Äã.‚Äã‚Äã 2A = B + C. But in a triangle, A + B + C = 180‚àò.180^\circ.180‚àò.‚Äã‚Äã Substitute B + C = 2A: A + 2A =180‚àò= 180^\circ=180‚àò‚Äã‚Äã 3A=180‚àò = 180^\circ=180‚àò‚Äã‚Äã A = 60‚àò.60^\circ.60‚àò.‚Äã‚Äã So, the triangle always has one angle = 60‚àò.60^\circ.60‚àò.

questions.question: A speaks the truth 3 out of 4 times, and B 5 out of 6 times, what is the probability that they will contradict each other in stating the same fact?
questions.explanation: Given: Probability that A speaks truth = 34\frac 3443‚Äã‚Äã, so probability that A lies = 14\frac 1441‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Probability that B speaks truth = 56\frac 5665‚Äã‚Äã, so probability that B lies = 16\frac 1661‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Formula Used: Contradiction happens if: A speaks truth & B lies A lies & B speaks truth P(Contradiction) = (P(A truth) √ó P(B lie)) + (P(A lie) √ó P(B truth)) Solution: =(34√ó16)+(14√ó56)=324+524=824=13= \left(\dfrac{3}{4} \times \dfrac{1}{6}\right) + \left(\dfrac{1}{4} \times \dfrac{5}{6}\right) \\[6pt]= \dfrac{3}{24} + \dfrac{5}{24} \\[6pt]= \dfrac{8}{24} = \dfrac{1}{3}=(43‚Äã√ó61‚Äã)+(41‚Äã√ó65‚Äã)=243‚Äã+245‚Äã=248‚Äã=31‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: Convert 34710347_{10}34710‚Äã‚Äã into octal.
questions.explanation: Given: Decimal number = 347 Solution: Divide 347 by 8 repeatedly: 347 √∑ 8 = 43 remainder 3 43 √∑ 8 = 5 remainder 3 5 √∑ 8 = 0 remainder 5 So 34710=5338347_{10}=533_{8}34710‚Äã=5338‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: A loan is to be repaid in two equal annual installments at 10 % p.a. compound interest.Can the exact amount of each installment be determined? I. The principal of the loan is ‚Çπ10 000. II. The total interest paid over the two years is ‚Çπ2 100.
options: Either I or II is sufficient. ‚Äã
questions.explanation: Solution: Principal = ‚Çπ10,000 Rate = 10% p.a. Time = 2 years Installments = 2 equal payments at the end of each year. Statement I: Let the installment amount be ‚Çπx. Then, the present value (PV) of these two installments, discounted at 10% p.a., should equal ‚Çπ10,000: ‚Äãx√ó(1(1.10)+1(1.10)2)=10,000x \times \left(\frac{1}{(1.10)} + \frac{1}{(1.10)^2}\right) = 10,000x√ó((1.10)1‚Äã+(1.10)21‚Äã)=10,000‚Äã‚Äã This is a single equation with one unknown (x), so x can be calculated. Statement I alone is sufficient. Statement II: Statement II: Total interest paid in two years = ‚Çπ2,100 Suppose P is unknown with i=0.10, The present value of two equal payments is A: P=A1+i+A(1+i)2=\frac{A}{1+i}+\frac{A}{(1+i)^2}=1+iA‚Äã+(1+i)2A‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã A=P(1.1)22.1=121210P=\frac{P(1.1)^2}{2.1}=\frac{121}{210}P=2.1P(1.1)2‚Äã=210121‚ÄãP‚Äã Total interest = 2A- P=2100 (242210‚àí1)P=2100\left(\frac{242}{210}-1\right)P=2100(210242‚Äã‚àí1)P=2100‚Äã‚Äã 16105P=2100\frac{16}{105}P=210010516‚ÄãP=2100‚Äã P=‚Çπ13,781.25 Then A=121210P=‚Çπ7,940.625\frac{121}{210}P=‚Çπ7{,}940.625210121‚ÄãP=‚Çπ7,940.625‚Äã Statement II alone is sufficient. ‚Äã

questions.question: M sold an article to N for Rs. 780 at a loss of 22%. N spent Rs. 240 on repairing it and sold it to R at a price at which M would have earned a profit of 25%. Find the profit earned by N?
questions.explanation: Given: M sold to N for ‚Çπ780 at a loss of 22%. N spent ‚Çπ240 on repair. N sold to R at a price at which M would have made a 25% profit. We are to find N's profit. Formula Used: Loss or profit is always calculated on Cost Price (CP). Use reverse calculation to find CP using: SP = CP√ó(1¬±Profit or Loss %100) \text{CP} \times \left(1 \pm \frac{\text{Profit or Loss \%}}{100}\right)CP√ó(1¬±100Profit or Loss %‚Äã)‚Äã‚Äã Profit = Selling Price ‚Äì Total Cost Price Solution: Given M sold at ‚Çπ780 at 22% loss: CP of M = 7801‚àí22100=7800.78\frac{780}{1 - \frac{22}{100}} = \frac{780}{0.78}1‚àí10022‚Äã780‚Äã=0.78780‚Äã‚Äã = ‚Çπ1000 SP at 25% profit = 1000√ó(1+25100)=1000√ó1.251000 \times \left(1 + \frac{25}{100} \right) = 1000 \times 1.251000√ó(1+10025‚Äã)=1000√ó1.25‚Äã = ‚Çπ1250 N‚Äôs total cost = 780 + 240 = ‚Çπ1020 N sold it for ‚Çπ1250 Profit of N = 1250 - 1020 = ‚Çπ230

questions.question: During Diwali sale, company A gives 'buy 4 sweaters and get 2 free' offer, company B gives 'buy 2 sweaters and get 3 free' offer, and company C gives a discount of 5%. Which of the following is true? 1. A gives the best offer for the customer. 2. B gives the best offer for the customer. 3. Both A and B actually give the same offer. 4. C gives the best offer.
questions.explanation: Solution: Company A: "Buy 4 sweaters and get 2 free." You pay for 4 sweaters and get 6 sweaters in total. Effective price per sweater = 4 sweaters‚Äô cost6 sweaters=23 of the original price \frac{4 \text{ sweaters' cost}}{6 \text{ sweaters}} = \frac{2}{3} \text{ of the original price}6 sweaters4 sweaters‚Äô cost‚Äã=32‚Äã of the original price‚Äã‚Äã Company B: "Buy 2 sweaters and get 3 free." You pay for 2 sweaters and get 5 sweaters in total. Effective price per sweater ‚Äã=2 sweaters‚Äô cost5 sweaters=25 of the original price= \frac{2 \text{ sweaters' cost}}{5 \text{ sweaters}} = \frac{2}{5} \text{ of the original price}=5 sweaters2 sweaters‚Äô cost‚Äã=52‚Äã of the original price‚Äã‚Äã Company C: "5% discount." You pay 95% of the original price for each sweater. Effective price per sweater = 0.95 of the original price Comparing the value; ‚Äã23‚âà0.6667\frac{2}{3} \approx 0.666732‚Äã‚âà0.6667‚Äã‚Äã 25\frac{2}{5}52‚Äã‚Äã = 0.4 0.95 = 0.95 Thus, Company B offers the best deal, as we pay the least per sweater (0.4, or 40% of the original price).

questions.question: Two trains of length 600 m each are moving in the same direction on parallel tracks. Initially train A is ahead of train B and both the trains are moving with the same speed of 108 km/h. The driver of train B accelerates by 1 m/sec2^22‚ÄãAfter 50 sec train B is wust completely ahead of train A. Calculate the original distance (in m) between trains A and B.‚Äã
questions.explanation: Given: Length of train A (L‚Çê) = Length of train B (L‚ÇÜ) = 600 m Speed of train A (u‚Çê) = Speed of train B (u‚ÇÜ) = 108 km/h Time to cross the train with acceleration 1 m/sec¬≤ = 50 sec Formula used: S = ut + (12\frac1221‚Äã‚Äã)at¬≤ Where, S = distance, u = speed of train t = time and a = acceleration Distance = Speed √ó Time Solution: u‚Çê = u‚ÇÜ = 108 km/h = 108 √ó 518\frac5{18}185‚Äã‚Äã m/s u‚Çê = u‚ÇÜ = 30 m/sec Distance traveled by B is S‚ÇÜ S‚ÇÜ = (30 √ó 50) + (12\frac1221‚Äã‚Äã) √ó 1 √ó 50¬≤ S‚ÇÜ = 1500 + 1250 S‚ÇÜ = 2750 m Let, the distance traveled by A is S‚Çê S‚Çê = u‚Çê √ó t S‚Çê = 30 √ó 50 = 1500 Original distance between the trains = S‚ÇÜ - S‚Çê S‚ÇÜ - S‚Çê = 2750 - 1500 = 1250

questions.question: Sonu and Monu together can build a wall in 6 days, while Monu and Mohan together can build the same wall in 12 days. Sonu and Monu work together for 5 days after which they both fall ill. The remaining work is completed by Mohan in 10 days. The total payment for the work done is ‚Çπ3,600 which is to be divided among three persons. What will be Sonu‚Äôs share out of the total payment?
questions.explanation: Given: Sonu and Monu can build a wall in 6 days Monu and Mohan can build the wall in 12 days Solution: Sonu and Monu in 5 days 5 √ó 2 = 10 units Remaining work = 12 - 10 = 2 units Remaining work is completed by Mohan in 10 days Efficiency of Mohan = 210\frac2{10}102‚Äã‚Äã = 0.2 units/day Efficiency of Monu + Mohan = 1 Efficiency of Monu = 1 - 0.2 = 0.8 Efficiency of Sonu + Monu = 2 Efficiency of Sonu = 2 - 0.8 = 1.2 Thus, Work done by Sonu in 5 days = 5 √ó 1.2 = 6 units Share of Sonu in payment = 3600 √ó 612\frac6{12}126‚Äã‚Äã = ‚Çπ1800

questions.question: Which of the following statements are not true? Statement 1: 15 mangoes are bought for Rs. 250, and 9 mangoes are bought for Rs. 200. If all the mangoes are sold at Rs. 240 per dozen, then profit percentage in the entire transaction is 6.67% (rounded to two decimal places)?. Statement 2: Single discount equivalent to two successive discounts of 15% and 5% will be 19.25%. Statement 3: The marked price of a table is Rs. 8,980. If the shopkeeper offers a discount of 19%, then the selling price would be Rs. 7000 (in Rs., rounded to the nearest rupee)? ‚Äã
questions.explanation: Statement 1: Given: 15 mangoes are bought for Rs. 250 9 mangoes are bought for Rs. 200 All the mangoes are sold at Rs. 240 per dozen (12 mangoes) Formula Used: Profit = Total SP - Total CP Profit Percentage =ProfitTotal CP√ó100 \frac{\text{Profit}}{\text{Total CP}} \times 100Total CPProfit‚Äã√ó100‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Cost of 15 mangoes = ‚Çπ250 Cost of 9 mangoes = ‚Çπ200 Total CP = ‚Çπ250 + ‚Çπ200 = ‚Çπ450 Selling price per dozen = ‚Çπ240 Selling price per mango = 24012\frac{240}{12}12240‚Äã‚Äã = ‚Çπ20 Total number of mangoes = 15 + 9 = 24 mangoes Total SP = 24 √ó ‚Çπ20 = ‚Çπ480 Now, Profit = ‚Çπ480 - ‚Çπ450 = ‚Çπ30 Profit Percentage = 30450√ó100=6.67%\frac{30}{450} \times 100 = 6.67\%45030‚Äã√ó100=6.67% Statement 1 is correct Statement 2: ‚ÄãGiven: ‚Äã2 successive discounts of 15% and 5%. ‚ÄãFormula Used: ‚ÄãEffective discount for a% and b% discounts = a% + b% - (ab/100)% ‚ÄãSolution: ‚ÄãEffective discount for 15% and 5% discounts = 15%+5%‚àí{15√ó5100}%=20%‚àí0.75%=19.25%15\% + 5\% - \left\{ \frac{15 \times 5}{100} \right\}\% = 20\% - 0.75\% = 19.25\%15%+5%‚àí{10015√ó5‚Äã}%=20%‚àí0.75%=19.25% Statement 2 is correct Statement 3: Given: Marked price (MP) = ‚Çπ8,980 Discount = 19% Formula Used: Selling Price (SP) = MP - Discount- Solution Calculate the discount amount: Discount = 8980√ó19100=1706.28980√ó\frac{19}{100}=1706.28980√ó10019‚Äã=1706.2‚Äã‚Äã Selling Price: SP =8980-1706.2= 7273.8 ‚âà7274\approx 7274‚âà7274 ‚âÝ7200\neq 7200ÓÄÝ=7200‚Äã‚Äã Statement 3 is not correct ‚Äã

questions.question: k is the greatest number which, when divides 2996, 4752 and 7825, the remainder in each case is the same. The product of the digits of k is
questions.explanation: Given: k is the greatest number which, when divides 2996, 4752 and 7825, the remainder in each case is the same. Formula Used: HCF - The greatest number which divides each of the two or more numbers Solution: According to the question =&gt (7825 ‚Äì 4752) = 3073 =&gt (7825 ‚Äì 2996) = 4829 =&gt (4752 ‚Äì 2996) = 1756 Now, HCF of 3073, 4829 and 1756 is =&gt 3073 = 7 √ó 439 =&gt 4829 = 11 √ó 439 =&gt 1756 = 2 √ó 2 √ó 439 So, k = 439 Now, The product of the digits of k = (4 √ó 3 √ó 9)= 108

questions.question: A boat takes 27 minutes to go 37.2 km upstream. The ratio of the speed of the boat in still water to that of the stream is 8 : 5. How much total time (in hours) will the boat take to go 79.4 km upstream and 85.8 km downstream?
questions.explanation: Given: Time to go 37.2 km upstream = 27 minutes The ratio of the speed of the boat in still water to that of the stream is 8 : 5 We need to find the total time to go 79.4 km upstream and 85.8 km downstream Formula Used: ‚Äã Speed = DistanceTime\frac{Distance }{Time}TimeDistance‚Äã Solution: ‚Äã Let speed of boat is B and speed of stream be S So, Speed of boat = 8x speed of stream = 5x Now, 8x - 5x = 37.2√ó6027=2483\frac{37.2\times 60 }{27} = \frac{248}{3}2737.2√ó60‚Äã=3248‚Äã km/h x = 2489\frac{248}{9} 9248‚Äãkm/h Time taken to cover 79.4 km upstream = 79.4√ó3248=0.96h\frac{79.4 \times 3}{ 248} = 0.96 h24879.4√ó3‚Äã=0.96h Time taken to cover 75.8 km downstrream; = 85.8√ó913√ó248=0.239hr\frac{85.8\times 9}{13 \times 248}= 0.239 hr13√ó24885.8√ó9‚Äã=0.239hr Now, total time = 0.96 + 0.23 = 1.19‚âà1.2hr\approx 1.2 hr‚âà1.2hr‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: Find the median from the following data. Age in years 15 - 25 25 - 35 35 - 45 45 - 55 55 - 65 65 - 75 75 - 85 No. of patients 11 18 22 30 40 16 33
questions.explanation: Given: Class Frequency Cumulative frequency 15 ‚Äì 25 11 11 25 ‚Äì 35 18 29 35 ‚Äì 45 22 51 45 ‚Äì 55 30 81 55 ‚Äì 65 40 121 65 ‚Äì 75 16 137 75 ‚Äì 85 33 170 Formula Used: Median for grouped data : Median = L + (N2‚àíFf)√óh\left( \frac{\frac{N}{2} - F}{f} \right) \times h(f2N‚Äã‚àíF‚Äã)√óh‚Äã‚Äã Where: L = lower boundary of the median class N = total frequency F = cumulative frequency before the median class f = frequency of the median class h = class width Solution: Total Frequency (N) = 11 + 18 + 22 + 30 + 40 + 16 + 33 = 170 Median Position = N2=1702\frac{N}{2} = \frac{170}{2}2N‚Äã=2170‚Äã‚Äã = 85 Median Class: 55 ‚Äì 65 Now, L = 55, F = 81, f = 40, h = 10 Median = 55 + (85‚àí8140)√ó10\left( \frac{85 - 81}{40} \right) \times 10(4085‚àí81‚Äã)√ó10‚Äã‚Äã = 55 + (440)√ó10\left( \frac{4}{40} \right) \times 10(404‚Äã)√ó10‚Äã‚Äã = 55 + 1 = 56 years

questions.question: The monthly incomes of A and B are Rs. 60000 and Rs. 48000 respectively. A saves 3313\frac{1}{3}31‚Äã‚Äã% of his income and B spends 60% of his income. By what percent should A's savings be increased such that the ratio of expenditures of A and B gets reversed?
questions.explanation: Given: A's monthly income = Rs. 60,000 B's monthly income = Rs. 48,000 A's savings = 33‚Öì% of his income B's expenditure = 60% of his income (which implies B saves 40%) Solution: A's Initial Savings =13√ó60,000 \frac{1}{3} \times 60,000 31‚Äã√ó60,000‚Äã= ‚Çπ 20,000 A's Initial Expenditure = 60,000 - 20,000 = ‚Çπ40,000 B's Initial Expenditure = 0.6√ó48,0000.6 \times 48,0000.6√ó48,000‚Äã = ‚Çπ28,800 B's Initial Savings = 48,000 - 28,800 = ‚Çπ 19,200 Initial Ratio (A : B) = 40,00028,800=4028.8=2518 \frac{40,000}{28,800} = \frac{40}{28.8} = \frac{25}{18}28,80040,000‚Äã=28.840‚Äã=1825‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Desired (Reversed) Ratio of Expenditures = 1825 \frac{18}{25}2518‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Let x be the Increase in A's Savings New Savings of A = 20,000 + x New Expenditure of A = 60,000 - (20,000 + x) = 40,000 - x B's Expenditure remains unchanged at Rs. 28,800. ‚ÄãNew Expenditure of AExpenditure of B=1825\frac{\text{New Expenditure of A}}{\text{Expenditure of B}} = \frac{18}{25}Expenditure of BNew Expenditure of A‚Äã=2518‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã40,000‚àíx28,800=1825\frac{40,000 - x}{28,800} = \frac{18}{25}28,80040,000‚àíx‚Äã=2518‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã40,000‚àíx=1825√ó28,80040,000 - x = \frac{18}{25} \times 28,80040,000‚àíx=2518‚Äã√ó28,800‚Äã‚Äã 40,000 - x = 20,736 x = 40,000 - 20,736 = 19,264 Percentage Increase = (19,26420,000)√ó100=96.32%\left( \frac{19,264}{20,000} \right) \times 100 = 96.32\%(20,00019,264‚Äã)√ó100=96.32%

questions.question: ‚ÄãIf cot‚Å°A=512 \cot A = \frac5{12}cotA=125‚Äã‚Äã the find the value of sec‚Å°A‚àícosec‚Å°Asec‚Å°A+cosec‚Å°A‚àí177\sqrt{\frac{\sec A - \cosec A}{\sec A + \cosec A}} - \sqrt{\frac{17}{7}}secA+cosecAsecA‚àícosecA‚Äã‚Äã‚àí717‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äã ‚Äãcot‚Å°A=512 \cot A = \frac5{12}cotA=125‚Äã Formula Used: cot‚Å°Œ∏=BasePerpendicular sec‚Å°Œ∏=HypotenuseBase cosec‚Å°Œ∏=HypotenusePerpendicular\cot \theta = \frac{Base}{Perpendicular} \\ \ \\ \sec \theta = \frac{Hypotenuse}{Base}\\ \ \\ \cosec \theta = \frac{Hypotenuse}{Perpendicular}cotŒ∏=PerpendicularBase‚Äã secŒ∏=BaseHypotenuse‚Äã cosecŒ∏=PerpendicularHypotenuse‚Äã Solution: ‚Äãcot‚Å°A=512 \cot A = \frac5{12}cotA=125‚Äã so, base = 5, perpendicular = 12 by pythagorean triplet; hypotenuse = 13 Now, Putting the values; ‚Äãsec‚Å°A‚àícosec‚Å°Asec‚Å°A+cosec‚Å°A‚àí177 =135‚àí1312135+1312‚àí177 =156‚àí6560156+6560‚àí177 =916022160‚àí177 =91221‚àí177 =717‚àí177 =7‚àí17119 =‚àí10119\sqrt{\frac{\sec A - \cosec A}{\sec A + \cosec A}} - \sqrt{\frac{17}{7}} \\ \ \\ =\sqrt{\frac{\frac{13}5 - \frac{13}{12}}{\frac{13}5 + \frac{13}{12}}} - \sqrt{\frac{17}{7}} \\ \ \\= \sqrt{\frac{\frac{156-65}{60} }{\frac{156+65}{60}}} - \sqrt{\frac{17}{7}} \\ \ \\ = \sqrt{\frac{\frac{91}{60} }{\frac{221}{60}}} - \sqrt{\frac{17}{7}} \\ \ \\ = \sqrt{\frac{91} {221}} - \sqrt{\frac{17}{7}} \\ \ \\ = \sqrt{\frac{7} {17}} - \sqrt{\frac{17}{7}} \\ \ \\ = \frac{7 - 17}{\sqrt{119}} \\ \ \\ = \frac{-10}{\sqrt{119}}secA+cosecAsecA‚àícosecA‚Äã‚Äã‚àí717‚Äã‚Äã =513‚Äã+1213‚Äã513‚Äã‚àí1213‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚àí717‚Äã‚Äã =60156+65‚Äã60156‚àí65‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚àí717‚Äã‚Äã =60221‚Äã6091‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚àí717‚Äã‚Äã =22191‚Äã‚Äã‚àí717‚Äã‚Äã =177‚Äã‚Äã‚àí717‚Äã‚Äã =119‚Äã7‚àí17‚Äã =119‚Äã‚àí10‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: The coordinates of the centroid of the triangle ABC whose vertices are A(-5, 7), B(-4, -5)and C(4, 5), is
questions.explanation: Given: Coordinates of A = (-5, 7) Coordinates of B = (-4, -5) Coordinates of C = (4, 5) Formula Used: The formula to find the coordinates of the centroid of a triangle is: Centroid (G) = (x1+x2+x33,y1+y2+y33)\left( \frac{x_1 + x_2 + x_3}{3}, \frac{y_1 + y_2 + y_3}{3} \right)(3x1‚Äã+x2‚Äã+x3‚Äã‚Äã,3y1‚Äã+y2‚Äã+y3‚Äã‚Äã)‚Äã‚Äã Where (x‚ÇÅ, y‚ÇÅ), (x‚ÇÇ, y‚ÇÇ), and (x‚ÇÉ, y‚ÇÉ) are the coordinates of the vertices of the triangle. Solution: Coordinates of A = (-5, 7) Coordinates of B = (-4, -5) Coordinates of C = (4, 5) Substitute the coordinates into the formula for the centroid: ‚Äãx=‚àí5+(‚àí4)+43=‚àí53 y=7+(‚àí5)+53=73x = \frac{-5 + (-4) + 4}{3} = \frac{-5}{3} \\\ \\y = \frac{7 + (-5) + 5}{3} = \frac{7}{3}x=3‚àí5+(‚àí4)+4‚Äã=3‚àí5‚Äã y=37+(‚àí5)+5‚Äã=37‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Therefore, the coordinates of the centroid are: (‚àí53,73\frac{-5}{3},\frac{7}{3}3‚àí5‚Äã,37‚Äã‚Äã)

questions.question: The centres of two circles are 36 cm apart. If the radii of these two circles are 15 cm and 9 cm, respectively, then what is the sum of the lengths (in cm) of a direct common tangent and a transverse common tangent of these two circles?
questions.explanation: Given: Distance between the centers of the two circles = 36 cm Radius of the first circle = 15 cm Radius of the second circle = 9 cm Concept Used: Length of the direct common tangent ‚ÄãLdirect=d2‚àí(r1‚àír2)2L_{\text{direct}} = \sqrt{d^2 - (r_1 - r_2)^2}Ldirect‚Äã=d2‚àí(r1‚Äã‚àír2‚Äã)2‚Äã‚Äã Length of the transverse common tangent ‚ÄãLtransverse=d2‚àí(r1+r2)2L_{\text{transverse}} = \sqrt{d^2 - (r_1 + r_2)^2}Ltransverse‚Äã=d2‚àí(r1‚Äã+r2‚Äã)2‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Direct common tangent: Ldirect=362‚àí(15‚àí9)2 Ldirect=1296‚àí62 Ldirect=1296‚àí36 Ldirect=1260L_{\text{direct}} = \sqrt{36^2 - (15 - 9)^2}\\ \ \\L_{\text{direct}} = \sqrt{1296 - 6^2}\\ \ \\L_{\text{direct}} = \sqrt{1296 - 36}\\ \ \\L_{\text{direct}} = \sqrt{1260}Ldirect‚Äã=362‚àí(15‚àí9)2‚Äã Ldirect‚Äã=1296‚àí62‚Äã Ldirect‚Äã=1296‚àí36‚Äã Ldirect‚Äã=1260‚Äã Transverse common tangent: Ltransverse=362‚àí(15+9)2 Ltransverse=1296‚àí242 Ltransverse=1296‚àí576 Ltransverse=720L_{\text{transverse}} = \sqrt{36^2 - (15 + 9)^2}\\ \ \\L_{\text{transverse}} = \sqrt{1296 - 24^2}\\ \ \\L_{\text{transverse}} = \sqrt{1296 - 576}\\ \ \\L_{\text{transverse}} = \sqrt{720}Ltransverse‚Äã=362‚àí(15+9)2‚Äã Ltransverse‚Äã=1296‚àí242‚Äã Ltransverse‚Äã=1296‚àí576‚Äã Ltransverse‚Äã=720‚Äã Sum of the lengths: Sum=Ldirect+Ltransverse=1260+720 2√ó2√ó3√ó3√ó5√ó7+2√ó2√ó2√ó2√ó3√ó3√ó5\text{Sum} = L_{\text{direct}} + L_{\text{transverse}} = \sqrt{1260}+\sqrt{720}\\ \ \\\sqrt{2\times2\times3\times3\times5\times7}+\sqrt{2\times2\times2\times2\times3\times3\times5}Sum=Ldirect‚Äã+Ltransverse‚Äã=1260‚Äã+720‚Äã 2√ó2√ó3√ó3√ó5√ó7‚Äã+2√ó2√ó2√ó2√ó3√ó3√ó5‚Äã ‚Äã=67√ó5+125 =65(7+2)=6\sqrt{7\times5}+12\sqrt5\\ \ \\=6\sqrt{5}(\sqrt7+2)=67√ó5‚Äã+125‚Äã =65‚Äã(7‚Äã+2)‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: If tan‚Å°Œ±=pq\tan\alpha = \frac{p}{q}tanŒ±=qp‚Äã, then also Œ±=6Œ≤\alpha = 6\betaŒ±=6Œ≤, Œ±\alphaŒ± being an acute angle , find the value of : 12{pcosec‚Å°2Œ≤‚àíqsec‚Å°2Œ≤}\frac{1}{2}\{p \cosec 2\beta - q \sec 2\beta\}21‚Äã{pcosec2Œ≤‚àíqsec2Œ≤}‚Äã‚Äã
questions.explanation: Given: tan‚Å°Œ±=pq\tan\alpha = \frac{p}{q}tanŒ±=qp‚Äã Œ±=6Œ≤\alpha = 6\betaŒ±=6Œ≤, Œ±\alphaŒ± being an acute angle. Concept Used: ‚Äã ‚Äãsin‚Å°(x‚àíy)=sin‚Å°x‚ãÖcos‚Å°y‚àísin‚Å°y‚ãÖcos‚Å°x sin‚Å°2x=2sin‚Å°x‚ãÖcos‚Å°x\sin(x-y) = \sin x \cdot \cos y - \sin y \cdot \cos x \\ \ \\ \sin2x = 2\sin x \cdot \cos xsin(x‚àíy)=sinx‚ãÖcosy‚àísiny‚ãÖcosx sin2x=2sinx‚ãÖcosx Solution: ‚Äã ‚Äãtan‚Å°Œ±=pq\tan\alpha = \frac{p}{q}tanŒ±=qp‚Äã By Pythagoras theorem Now in the expression Multiplying by p2+q2p2+q2\frac{\sqrt{p^2+q^2}}{\sqrt{p^2 +q^2}}p2+q2‚Äãp2+q2‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã‚Äã=1(p2+q2)2{pp2+q2cosec‚Å°2Œ≤‚àíqp2+q2sec‚Å°2Œ≤} =1(p2+q2)2{sin‚Å°Œ±sin‚Å°2Œ≤‚àícos‚Å°Œ±cos‚Å°2Œ≤} =1(p2+q2)2{sin‚Å°Œ±‚ãÖcos‚Å°2Œ≤‚àícos‚Å°Œ±‚ãÖsin‚Å°2Œ≤sin‚Å°2Œ≤‚ãÖcos‚Å°2Œ≤} =p2+q2{sin‚Å°(Œ±‚àí2Œ≤)sin‚Å°4Œ≤}(Œ±=6Œ≤) =p2+q2{sin‚Å°(6Œ≤‚àí2Œ≤)sin‚Å°4Œ≤} =p2+q2{sin‚Å°(4Œ≤)sin‚Å°4Œ≤} =p2+q2=\frac{1(\sqrt{p^2 +q^2})}{2}\{\frac{p}{\sqrt{p^2 +q^2}} \cosec 2\beta - \frac{q}{\sqrt{p^2 +q^2}} \sec 2\beta\} \\ \ \\ =\frac{1(\sqrt{p^2 +q^2})}{2}\{\frac{\sin \alpha} {\sin 2\beta} - \frac{\cos \alpha} {\cos 2\beta}\}\\ \ \\ =\frac{1(\sqrt{p^2 +q^2})}{2}\{\frac{\sin \alpha \cdot \cos 2\beta-\cos \alpha\cdot \sin2\beta } {\sin 2\beta\cdot \cos 2 \beta} \}\\ \ \\ =\sqrt{p^2 +q^2}\{\frac{\sin (\alpha - 2\beta) } {\sin 4\beta} \} \quad (\alpha = 6\beta)\\ \ \\ =\sqrt{p^2 +q^2}\{\frac{\sin (6\beta - 2\beta) } {\sin 4\beta} \} \\ \ \\ =\sqrt{p^2 +q^2}\left\{\frac{\cancel{\sin (4\beta )} } {\cancel{\sin 4\beta}}\right \} \\ \ \\ =\sqrt{p^2 +q^2}=21(p2+q2‚Äã)‚Äã{p2+q2‚Äãp‚Äãcosec2Œ≤‚àíp2+q2‚Äãq‚Äãsec2Œ≤} =21(p2+q2‚Äã)‚Äã{sin2Œ≤sinŒ±‚Äã‚àícos2Œ≤cosŒ±‚Äã} =21(p2+q2‚Äã)‚Äã{sin2Œ≤‚ãÖcos2Œ≤sinŒ±‚ãÖcos2Œ≤‚àícosŒ±‚ãÖsin2Œ≤‚Äã} =p2+q2‚Äã{sin4Œ≤sin(Œ±‚àí2Œ≤)‚Äã}(Œ±=6Œ≤) =p2+q2‚Äã{sin4Œ≤sin(6Œ≤‚àí2Œ≤)‚Äã} =p2+q2‚Äã{sin4Œ≤ ‚Ä¢ ‚Äãsin(4Œ≤) ‚Ä¢ ‚Äã‚Äã} =p2+q2‚Äã ‚Äã

questions.question: If 43x‚àí8x+1+16=04^{3x} - 8^{x+1} + 16 = 043x‚àí8x+1+16=0 is written as a quadratic equation where y=23xy = 2^{3x}y=23x , then which of the options below will represent the quadratic equation mentioned above? ‚Äã ‚Äã‚Äã
There are 20 people in a party. If every person shakes hand with every other person, then what will be the total number of handshakes?
questions.explanation: Given: Number of people in the party = 20 Formula Used: The number of handshakes in a group of 'n' people where everyone shakes hands with everyone else is given by: n √ón‚àí12\times \frac{n-1}{2}√ó2n‚àí1‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Apply the formula: Number of handshakes = 20 √ó20‚àí12\times \frac{20-1}{2}√ó220‚àí1‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Number of handshakes = 20 √ó192\times \frac{19}{2}√ó219‚Äã‚Äã Number of handshakes = 190 Therefore, there will be a total of 190 handshakes. Option (d) is right.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct statement?
options: The sum of the angles of a cyclic quadrilateral is always 180¬∞.
Equal chords are equidistant from the centre of the circle but not always subtend equal angle at centre of the circle
If two circles touch each other, the point of contact lies on the line woining the two centres.
Angles subtended by the arc in the same segment of the circle are in ratio of 2 : 1.
questions.explanation: If two circles touch each other, the point of contact lies on the line woining the two centres. Correct. When two circles touch each other (either externally or internally), the point of contact lies on the line woining their centers. Here's a brief explanation of each statement: (a) The sum of the angles of a cyclic quadrilateral is always 180¬∞. Incorrect. The sum of the angles of a cyclic quadrilateral is 360¬∞, not 180¬∞. (b) Equal chords are equidistant from the centre of the circle but not always subtend equal angle at centre of the circle. Incorrect. Equal chords are equidistant from the center of the circle and always subtend equal angles at the center. (d) Angles subtended by the arc in the same segment of the circle are in ratio of 2 : 1. Incorrect. Angles subtended by the same arc in the same segment of the circle are equal, so the ratio would be 1:1, not 2:1.

questions.question: The ratio of the diameters of two spheres is given as 1 : 4. The larger sphere is melted and 125 identical spheres are made out of the molten material. The smaller sphere is melted and 27 identical spheres are made out of the molten material. If the ratio of the volume of each of the 125 identical spheres to the volume of each of the 27 identical spheres is given as 1 : m, what is the value of m?
questions.explanation: Given: Ratio of the diameters = 1 : 4 Formula Used: Volume of sphere = 43œÄr3\frac43 œÄr^334‚ÄãœÄr3‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Let the radius of the small spheres be r The radius of the larger sphere be R The radius of melted sphere be x As per the question, ‚Äã43œÄx3=125‚ãÖ43œÄ(4R)3x3=125‚ãÖ(4R)3x3(4R)3=125x4R=5R=4x5\frac{4}{3} \pi x^3 = 125 \cdot \frac{4}{3} \pi (4R)^3\\x^3 = 125 \cdot (4R)^3\\\frac{x^3}{(4R)^3} = 125\\\frac{x}{4R} = 5\\R = \frac{4x}{5}\\34‚ÄãœÄx3=125‚ãÖ34‚ÄãœÄ(4R)3x3=125‚ãÖ(4R)3(4R)3x3‚Äã=1254Rx‚Äã=5R=54x‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Similarly, ‚Äã43œÄx3=27‚ãÖ43œÄr3x3=27‚ãÖr3x3r3=27xr=3r=x3\frac{4}{3} \pi x^3 = 27 \cdot \frac{4}{3} \pi r^3\\x^3 = 27 \cdot r^3\\\frac{x^3}{r^3} = 27\\\frac{x}{r} = 3\\r = \frac{x}{3}\\34‚ÄãœÄx3=27‚ãÖ34‚ÄãœÄr3x3=27‚ãÖr3r3x3‚Äã=27rx‚Äã=3r=3x‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã As per the question, ‚Äã43œÄR343œÄr3=1mPutting the values of R and r:43œÄ(4x5)343œÄ(x3)3=1m(4x5)3(x3)3=1m64x3125x327=1m64125√ó27=1mm=12564√ó27m=1251728\frac{\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3}{\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3} = \frac{1}{m}\\\text{Putting the values of } R \text{ and } r:\\\frac{\frac{4}{3} \pi \left(\frac{4x}{5}\right)^3}{\frac{4}{3} \pi \left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^3} = \frac{1}{m}\\\frac{\left(\frac{4x}{5}\right)^3}{\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^3} = \frac{1}{m}\\\frac{\frac{64x^3}{125}}{\frac{x^3}{27}} = \frac{1}{m}\\\frac{64}{125} \times 27 = \frac{1}{m}\\m = \frac{125}{64 \times 27}\\m = \frac{125}{1728}\\34‚ÄãœÄr334‚ÄãœÄR3‚Äã=m1‚ÄãPutting the values of R and r:34‚ÄãœÄ(3x‚Äã)334‚ÄãœÄ(54x‚Äã)3‚Äã=m1‚Äã(3x‚Äã)3(54x‚Äã)3‚Äã=m1‚Äã27x3‚Äã12564x3‚Äã‚Äã=m1‚Äã12564‚Äã√ó27=m1‚Äãm=64√ó27125‚Äãm=1728125‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: The mean and standard deviation of 100 observations were calculated as 40 and 5.1 respectively by a student who took 50 instead of 40 for one observation. What is the correct mean and standard deviation?
questions.explanation: Solution: ‚ÄãMean=40=&gtŒ£i=1100xi100=40Mean = 40 \Rightarrow \frac{\Sigma_{i=1}^{100} x_i}{100} = 40Mean=40=&gt100Œ£i=1100‚Äãxi‚Äã‚Äã=40‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã=&gtŒ£i=1100xi=4000\Rightarrow \Sigma_{i=1}^{100} x_i = 4000=&gtŒ£i=1100‚Äãxi‚Äã=4000‚Äã‚Äã ‚ÄãSD=5.1=&gtœÉ2=(5.1)2 =&gtŒ£i=1100xi2100‚àí(40)2=26.01 =&gtŒ£i=1100xi2100=162601 Thus, incorrectŒ£i=1100xi=4000 and incorrectŒ£i=1100xi2=162601 Now, incorrectŒ£i=1100xi=4000 CorrectŒ£i=1100xi2={162601‚àí(50)2+(40)2}=161701 Correct variance=CorrectŒ£i=1100xi2100‚àí(Correct Mean)2 =161701100‚àí(39.9)2 =(1617.01)‚àí(40‚àí0.1)2 =(1617.01)‚àí(1600+0.01√ó8) =(1617.01‚àí1592.01)=25 Correct SD=25=5 Hence, correct mean = 39.9 and correct SD = 5.\text{SD} = 5.1 \Rightarrow \sigma^2 = (5.1)^2\\\ \\\Rightarrow \frac{\Sigma_{i=1}^{100} x_i^2}{100} - (40)^2 = 26.01\\\ \\\Rightarrow \frac{\Sigma_{i=1}^{100} x_i^2}{100} = 162601\\\ \\\text{Thus, incorrect} \Sigma_{i=1}^{100} x_i = 4000 \text{ and incorrect} \Sigma_{i=1}^{100} x_i^2 = 162601\\\ \\\text{Now, incorrect} \Sigma_{i=1}^{100} x_i = 4000\\\ \\\text{Correct} \Sigma_{i=1}^{100} x_i^2 = \{162601 - (50)^2 + (40)^2\} = 161701\\\ \\\text{Correct variance} = \frac{\text{Correct} \Sigma_{i=1}^{100} x_i^2}{100} - (\text{Correct Mean})^2\\\ \\= \frac{161701}{100} - (39.9)^2\\\ \\= (1617.01) - (40 - 0.1)^2\\\ \\= (1617.01) - (1600 + 0.01 \times 8)\\\ \\= (1617.01 - 1592.01) = 25\\\ \\\text{Correct SD} = \sqrt{25} = 5 \\\ \\\text{Hence, correct mean = 39.9 and correct SD = 5.}SD=5.1=&gtœÉ2=(5.1)2 =&gt100Œ£i=1100‚Äãxi2‚Äã‚Äã‚àí(40)2=26.01 =&gt100Œ£i=1100‚Äãxi2‚Äã‚Äã=162601 Thus, incorrectŒ£i=1100‚Äãxi‚Äã=4000 and incorrectŒ£i=1100‚Äãxi2‚Äã=162601 Now, incorrectŒ£i=1100‚Äãxi‚Äã=4000 CorrectŒ£i=1100‚Äãxi2‚Äã={162601‚àí(50)2+(40)2}=161701 Correct variance=100CorrectŒ£i=1100‚Äãxi2‚Äã‚Äã‚àí(Correct Mean)2 =100161701‚Äã‚àí(39.9)2 =(1617.01)‚àí(40‚àí0.1)2 =(1617.01)‚àí(1600+0.01√ó8) =(1617.01‚àí1592.01)=25 Correct SD=25‚Äã=5 Hence, correct mean = 39.9 and correct SD = 5.‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: The circumference of the base of the cylindrical vessel is 154 cm and its height is 49 mm. How many litres of water can it hold? (correct to three places of decimals, use œÄ = 22/7)
questions.explanation: Given: Circumference of the base of the cylinder = 154 cm Height of the cylinder = 49 mm = 4.9 cm (since 1 cm = 10 mm) ‚ÄãœÄ=227\pi = \frac{22}{7}œÄ=722‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Formula Used: The formula for the volume of a cylinder is: ‚ÄãV=œÄr2hV = \pi r^2 hV=œÄr2h‚Äã‚Äã Where: r is the radius of the base of the cylinder, h is the height of the cylinder. Circumference of a circle = 2œÄr Solution: ‚Äã154=2√ó227√ór154 = 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times r 154=2√ó722‚Äã√ór‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã154=447√ór154 = \frac{44}{7} \times r154=744‚Äã√ór‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äãr=154√ó744=107844=24.5 cmr = \frac{154 \times 7}{44} = \frac{1078}{44} = 24.5 \text{ cm}r=44154√ó7‚Äã=441078‚Äã=24.5 cm‚Äã‚Äã Now, using the formula for the volume of a cylinder: ‚ÄãV=227√ó(24.5)2√ó4.9V = \frac{22}{7} \times (24.5)^2 \times 4.9V=722‚Äã√ó(24.5)2√ó4.9‚Äã‚Äã ‚ÄãV=227√ó600.25√ó4.9V = \frac{22}{7} \times 600.25 \times 4.9V=722‚Äã√ó600.25√ó4.9‚Äã‚Äã ‚ÄãV=227√ó2941.225=9243.85 cubic cmV = \frac{22}{7} \times 2941.225 = 9243.85 \text{ cubic cm}V=722‚Äã√ó2941.225=9243.85 cubic cm‚Äã‚Äã Now, Since 1 liter = 1000 cubic cm, the Volume in liters is: ‚ÄãV=9243.851000‚âà9.244 litersV = \frac{9243.85}{1000} \approx \bf9.244 \text{ liters}V=10009243.85‚Äã‚âà9.244 liters‚Äã‚Äã
